title: Write 'How to Add a New OS Concept' contributor guide with step-by-step instructions
description: No documentation exists for adding a new concept to the OS module. A contributor must reverse-engineer the pattern from a 3933-line file. Write a guide covering: (1) which files to create (engine in src/lib/os/), (2) which engine contract to follow (input→events→result), (3) the 7 registration points in OSModule.tsx (type union, CONCEPTS array, state, callback, canvas, properties, event log), (4) how to test, (5) checklist before PR. This saves 4-6 hours per new contributor.
acceptanceCriteria: Guide exists at src/lib/os/README.md or docs/os-concepts/CONTRIBUTING.md
Step-by-step: create engine → add types → register in UI → add tests
Lists ALL 7 registration points in OSModule.tsx with line numbers
Includes annotated template for a minimal engine file
Includes minimal test template
Checklist: 'New OS Concept Checklist' with 10+ items

title: Create OS concept scaffolding script — pnpm scaffold:os-concept
description: No scaffolding exists. Adding a new concept requires creating an engine file, updating index.ts, updating 7 places in OSModule.tsx, and creating tests. Build a script that generates the boilerplate: `pnpm scaffold:os-concept --name disk-scheduling --algorithms sstf,scan,cscan,look`. Creates engine file with template, updates index.ts, prints remaining manual steps.
acceptanceCriteria: Script at scripts/scaffold-os-concept.ts
Creates src/lib/os/{name}.ts with engine boilerplate
Creates src/__tests__/lib/os/{name}.test.ts with test boilerplate
Updates src/lib/os/index.ts with new exports
Prints checklist of manual steps (UI registration points)
Available via: pnpm scaffold:os-concept --name disk-scheduling

title: Add JSDoc to all exported functions in src/lib/os/ — @param, @returns, @example
description: JSDoc coverage: memory.ts has 9 tags, most files have 0. Add @param, @returns, and @example to every exported function. This enables IDE tooltips, auto-generated API docs, and helps contributors understand the engine contract without reading implementation. 19 total @param/@returns instances is woefully insufficient for 8 engine files with 30+ exported functions.
acceptanceCriteria: Every exported function has @param for each parameter
Every exported function has @returns describing return type
At least 10 functions have @example with runnable code
JSDoc visible in IDE tooltips when hovering over imports
Function-level doc comments explain the algorithm in 1-2 sentences

title: Consolidate test locations — move all OS tests to src/lib/os/__tests__/
description: OS tests are split between src/__tests__/lib/os/ (scheduling, page-replacement, deadlock, mlfq-scheduler) and src/lib/os/__tests__/ (bankers-algorithm). A contributor doesn't know which location to use. Consolidate all to src/lib/os/__tests__/ to co-locate tests with source.
acceptanceCriteria: All OS test files in src/lib/os/__tests__/
src/__tests__/lib/os/ directory removed or empty
Vitest config still finds tests
CI pipeline passes

title: Write architecture overview document for OS module with data flow diagram
description: No architecture documentation exists. A new contributor has no map of the 16-file, 8553-line module. Write an architecture overview covering: (1) file inventory with purposes, (2) data flow from user input to rendering, (3) how concepts are composed (sidebar+canvas+properties+bottomPanel), (4) engine contract (what engines must return), (5) how to extend.
acceptanceCriteria: Architecture doc at src/lib/os/ARCHITECTURE.md
File inventory: each file, its purpose, line count
Data flow diagram (Mermaid): user input → engine → result → rendering
Engine contract: what every engine function must accept and return
Extension guide: how adding a new concept flows through the architecture

title: Add pre-commit hooks with lint, typecheck, and test-changed for OS module
description: No pre-commit hooks exist (no husky or lint-staged). Broken code can be committed and push to CI to fail. Add: lint-staged with ESLint + Prettier on staged .ts/.tsx files, tsc --noEmit type check, and vitest related on changed files. This catches issues before they reach CI.
acceptanceCriteria: husky + lint-staged installed and configured
Pre-commit: eslint --fix on staged files
Pre-commit: prettier --write on staged files
Pre-push: tsc --noEmit (full type check)
Pre-push: vitest related (tests for changed files only)

title: Create PR review checklist for OS module contributions
description: No quality criteria exist for new OS concept contributions. Different concepts have wildly different quality levels (Belady's Anomaly = excellent, Mutex = minimal). Create a PR review checklist that defines done: config metadata, engine registered, step descriptions explain WHY, tests exist, edge cases handled, no TypeScript any, complexity verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Checklist in .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE/os-concepts.md
Minimum 12 checkboxes covering: engine, types, UI, tests, content quality
Auto-applied when PR modifies src/lib/os/ or src/components/modules/os/
Key items: 'Step descriptions explain WHY, not just WHAT', 'No any types', 'Edge cases: empty, single, max'
